decks.notes: This deck focuses on quickly overwhelming the opponent with aggressive creatures and disrupting their hand with discard spells. The goal is to apply constant pressure by playing efficient threats like Hypnotic Specter and Nantuko Shade while removing opposing threats with cards like Snuff Out and Smother. Duress and Cabal Therapy help to strip the opponent's hand of key cards, allowing for a smoother path to victory. Mishra's Factory and Wasteland provide additional utility in the form of creature and land destruction. The deck aims to establish board control early and maintain it until the opponent runs out of resources.

The main idea behind this Premodern 1 vs 1 Aggro - Sligh (RDW) deck is to overwhelm the opponent with fast, efficient creatures and burn spells. By utilizing cheap, aggressive creatures like Jackal Pup and Mogg Fanatic, along with powerful burn spells like Lightning Bolt and Fireblast, the deck aims to quickly reduce the opponent's life total to zero. With cards like Ball Lightning and Sulfuric Vortex adding additional pressure, this deck is focused on delivering consistent, aggressive damage to secure a swift victory.

This deck focuses on quickly overwhelming the opponent with a swarm of aggressive Goblin creatures. By utilizing cards like Goblin Lackey and Goblin Matron to search for key cards like Goblin Piledriver and Siege-Gang Commander, the deck aims to build a formidable board presence early in the game. Rishadan Port and Wasteland provide disruption for the opponent's mana base, while Gempalm Incinerator offers removal options for opposing creatures. The deck's strategy revolves around efficiently deploying Goblins and utilizing their synergies to put pressure on the opponent and secure a swift victory.

The deck focuses on quickly putting powerful creatures from the graveyard onto the battlefield by using cards like Reanimate and Exhume. With creatures like Verdant Force, Symbiotic Wurm, and Akroma, Angel of Wrath, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with big threats early in the game. Cards like Unmask and Cabal Therapy help to disrupt the opponent's hand and strategy, allowing the deck to execute its combo smoothly. Dark Ritual and Life/Death provide additional mana acceleration and flexibility in reanimating creatures. Overall, the deck seeks to win through a fast and hard-hitting reanimation strategy.

The deck focuses on controlling the game by using a combination of discard spells, removal spells, and sweepers to disrupt the opponent's strategy. By managing the opponent's resources and board presence, the deck aims to outvalue the opponent and ultimately dominate the late game. With a mix of card advantage engines, board wipes, and versatile removal, the deck can handle a wide range of threats and maintain stable control of the battlefield. With access to powerful cards like Vindicate and Decree of Justice, the deck can efficiently deal with any threats and establish dominance over the game.

The main focus of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent quickly with low-cost, aggressive creatures and a burn package designed to finish them off before they can stabilize. With a mix of creatures like Jackal Pup, Grim Lavamancer, and Ball Lightning, alongside burn spells like Lightning Bolt, Incinerate, and Fireblast, this deck aims to deal damage fast and efficiently. With a land base that helps to fix mana and a few pesky cards like Cursed Scroll and Sulfuric Vortex to add extra pressure, this deck is all about being fast and furious.

The main focus of this deck is to utilize the combination of Phyrexian Dreadnought and Stifle to create a powerful synergy. By cheating out the Dreadnought with Stifle's ability to counter the sacrifice trigger, the deck can quickly overwhelm opponents with a massive creature that is difficult to deal with. The deck also includes various card draw spells and counterspells to help maintain control and assemble the combo efficiently. Overall, the goal is to assemble the combo and disrupt opponents' strategies to secure a win through a big creature finish.

This aggro deck focuses on quickly establishing board presence with efficient creatures like Nimble Mongoose and Terravore, while disrupting opponents' mana bases with cards like Wasteland and Armageddon. The deck also utilizes powerful land destruction effects to control the battlefield and gradually increase the power of the Terravore. By combining aggressive creatures with land destruction tactics, this deck aims to overwhelm opponents and secure victory before they can recover.

The main focus of this deck is to aggressively attack the opponent using low-cost creatures like Hypnotic Specter and Knight of Stromgald, supported by removal spells like Smother and Duress to control the board. The deck also aims to disrupt the opponent's hand with cards like Cabal Therapy and Duress, while generating card advantage with Graveborn Muse. The inclusion of Mishra's Factory and Wasteland helps to keep the opponent's mana base in check. Overall, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent quickly with a mix of creature aggression and disruption.

The deck revolves around using Psychatog as a win condition while controlling the game through a combination of counterspells, discard spells, and card draw. The deck aims to take advantage of Psychatog's ability to grow in power by discarding cards and utilizing spells like Gush and Accumulated Knowledge to maintain card advantage. It also includes removal spells like Smother and Powder Keg to deal with opposing threats. Overall, the deck focuses on disruption and control to outlast opponents and win through a powerful creature.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly assemble the combo of Phyrexian Dreadnought and Stifle to create a powerful threat that can be difficult for opponents to deal with. The deck includes a variety of card draw and counter spells to help find and protect the combo pieces, while also including disruption in the form of Daze, Misdirection, and Thwart. The goal is to efficiently control the game while setting up the combo win condition.

The main concept of this deck is to use the Enchantress creatures and Enchantress's Presence to draw cards whenever an enchantment enters the battlefield. The deck focuses on ramping up mana quickly with cards like Wild Growth and Serra's Sanctum, while also controlling the board with cards like Parallax Wave and Swords to Plowshares. The key combo involves using Opalescence to turn all enchantments, including Parallax Wave, into creatures to control the board and win the game. The deck aims to outvalue and outpace the opponent through enchantment synergy and card draw.

This deck focuses on quickly ramping up mana, using land destruction to disrupt the opponent's resources, and then finishing them off with powerful land creatures like Terravore. By aggressively attacking the opponent's mana base and overwhelming them with threats, this deck aims to maintain control of the game and keep the pressure on the opponent. Devastating Dreams is a key card in the deck, allowing for a big blowout play that can often seal the deal and secure victory. Overall, the strategy is to be fast, disruptive, and relentless in its pursuit of victory.

The main focus of this deck is to control the board and disrupt the opponent's game plan through removal spells like Swords to Plowshares, Vindicate, and Gerrard's Verdict, as well as hand disruption with Duress and Cabal Therapy. The deck also aims to generate card advantage and inevitability with cards like Eternal Dragon, Decree of Justice, and Skeletal Scrying. The inclusion of a few creatures like Exalted Angel and Undead Gladiator provide alternate win conditions if needed. The overall strategy is to outlast the opponent and control the game until victory.

The key concept of this control deck is to disrupt the opponent's strategy through hand disruption with cards like Cabal Therapy and Duress, while also dealing with threats on the board using removal spells like Swords to Plowshares and Vindicate. The deck also aims to out-value the opponent with grindy cards like Eternal Dragon and Skeletal Scrying. By controlling the board and hand, the deck can then finish the game with powerful finishers like Decree of Justice and Decree of Pain, ultimately overwhelming the opponent with a combination of disruption and card advantage.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly ramp up mana and flood the board with Elves to overwhelm the opponent. By utilizing cards like Priest of Titania and Gaea's Cradle, the deck aims to generate large amounts of mana to cast powerful creatures like Deranged Hermit and Kamahl, Fist of Krosa. The inclusion of cards like Survival of the Fittest and Wirewood Symbiote allows for flexibility and card advantage, while creatures like Nantuko Vigilante and Caller of the Claw provide disruption and additional threats. The deck aims to maintain pressure and tempo to secure victory.
